metadata.title: Texas Instruments LM3S6965
metadata.description: HAIR extraction for the Texas Instruments LM3S6965 with core structure, physical pin/routing facts, and Embassy HAL profile coverage limited to the evidence-backed first-cut subset requested for lm3s6965evb.

evidence.name: Exact target identity
evidence.normalizedClaim: The device is the Texas Instruments Stellaris LM3S6965 Cortex-M3 microcontroller used by the lm3s6965evb board context.

evidence.name: Cortex-M3 core facts
evidence.normalizedClaim: The device header defines Cortex-M3 revision 0x0202, MPU present, 3 NVIC priority bits, and standard SysTick configuration.

evidence.name: Device IRQ inventory
evidence.normalizedClaim: The LM3S6965 device header enumerates the device IRQ inventory including GPIOA-F, UART0/1, SSI0, I2C0, WATCHDOG0, TIMER0-3A/B, SYSCTL, FLASH_CTRL, and SysTick.

evidence.name: Core memory map slices
evidence.normalizedClaim: The device memory map places flash at 0x00000000, SRAM at 0x20000000, peripheral space at 0x40000000, and Cortex-M3 private peripherals at 0xE000E000.

evidence.name: SysTick offsets
evidence.normalizedClaim: SysTick control, reload, and current registers are located at offsets 0x010, 0x014, and 0x018 within the Cortex-M3 private peripheral region.

evidence.name: CMSIS register blocks
evidence.normalizedClaim: The LM3S6965 CMSIS header provides register-bearing structural layouts for WATCHDOG0, GPIOA, SSI0, UART0, I2C0, TIMER0, FLASH_CTRL, and SYSCTL, and clones GPIO/UART/TIMER instance types across the family.

evidence.name: GPIO offsets and fields
evidence.normalizedClaim: TI-derived GPIO hardware header provides register offsets and bit masks for GPIO direction, interrupt, pad configuration, alternate-function select, and digital enable registers.

evidence.name: UART offsets and fields
evidence.normalizedClaim: TI-derived UART hardware header provides register offsets and bit masks for UART data, status, baud, line control, control, and interrupt registers.

evidence.name: SSI offsets and fields
evidence.normalizedClaim: TI-derived SSI hardware header provides register offsets and bit masks for SSI control, data, status, prescale, and interrupt registers.

evidence.name: I2C offsets and fields
evidence.normalizedClaim: TI-derived I2C hardware header provides register offsets and bit masks for I2C master/slave address, control/status, data, timing, configuration, and interrupt registers.

evidence.name: GPTM offsets and fields
evidence.normalizedClaim: TI-derived GPTM hardware header provides register offsets and bit masks for configuration, mode, control, load, match, prescale, counter, and interrupt registers.

evidence.name: Watchdog offsets and fields
evidence.normalizedClaim: TI-derived watchdog hardware header provides register offsets and bit masks for watchdog load, control, interrupt status/clear, test, and lock registers.

evidence.name: System control offsets and fields
evidence.normalizedClaim: TI-derived system control header provides register offsets and gating/reset field masks for GPIOA-F, UART0/1, SSI0, I2C0, TIMER0-3, and WDT0 along with RCC and RCC2 fields.

evidence.name: Requested peripheral pin routes
evidence.normalizedClaim: The datasheet pin tables place UART0 on PA0/PA1, SSI0 on PA2/PA3/PA4/PA5, I2C0 on PB2/PB3, UART1 on PD2/PD3, and CCP0-3 on PD4/PD7/PD5/PC6 for the 100-pin LQFP package.

evidence.name: Basic timer modes
evidence.normalizedClaim: Each GPTM supports 32-bit and split 16-bit one-shot and periodic timers, along with capture, PWM, and RTC modes; LM3S6965 timer timeout behavior is explicit for one-shot and periodic down-counter operation.

evidence.name: Timer API semantics
evidence.normalizedClaim: The Driver Library guide documents GPTM configure, enable/disable, interval load, and interrupt behavior and notes that some up-count variants are not available on all parts.

evidence.name: Clock enable timing caveat
evidence.normalizedClaim: Software must allow the peripheral clock gate to propagate before first access after peripheral enable.

evidence.name: Package type
evidence.normalizedClaim: LM3S6965 is offered in a 100-pin RoHS-compliant LQFP package in the referenced datasheet package tables.

device.name: LM3S6965
device.description: Concrete LM3S6965 extraction anchored to the lm3s6965evb MCU target.

memoryMap.name: PPB
memoryMap.description: Cortex-M3 private peripheral block slice for NVIC, SysTick, MPU, and SCB.

peripherals.name: NVIC
peripherals.description: Nested Vectored Interrupt Controller within the Cortex-M3 private peripheral block.

peripherals.name: WATCHDOG0
peripherals.description: 32-bit watchdog timer with first-timeout interrupt and second-timeout reset support.

UART1 shares the UART0 register model but uses PD2/PD3 pin routes; the shared CMSIS type is materialized into UART1-local register ids for lowering.

peripherals.name: I2C0
peripherals.description: Inter-integrated circuit controller 0 with master and slave sub-blocks.

peripherals.name: TIMER0
peripherals.description: General-purpose timer module 0 with Timer A and Timer B halves.

peripherals.name: TIMER1
peripherals.description: General-purpose timer module 1 with Timer A and Timer B halves.

provenance.notes: Timer1 shares the TIMER0 register model; CCP1 route is package-specific, so the shared type is materialized into TIMER1-local register ids for lowering.

peripherals.name: TIMER2
peripherals.description: General-purpose timer module 2 with Timer A and Timer B halves.

provenance.notes: Timer2 shares the TIMER0 register model; CCP2 route is package-specific, so the shared type is materialized into TIMER2-local register ids for lowering.

peripherals.name: TIMER3
peripherals.description: General-purpose timer module 3 with Timer A and Timer B halves.

provenance.notes: Timer3 shares the TIMER0 register model; CCP3 route is package-specific, so the shared type is materialized into TIMER3-local register ids for lowering.
